commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4791d3eb744934f666afc141f82b4060b4b0bdf3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR centralizes Control UI approval prompt cleanup and dismissal, moves Discord exec approval button acknowledgement earlier, and changes model manifest/default alias normalization paths.

PR surface: Source +328, Tests +579. Total +907 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main keeps stale resolve failures in execApprovalError and only removes resolved events from the queue. I did not run a pre-fix failing browser or Discord scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer or author handling is needed because the PR is protected and dirty, and the remaining work combines a line-level Discord response fix with compatibility-sensitive rebase review.

target.title: Fix stale approval prompts in Control UI
target.body:
Summary:
- Fix stale Control UI approval prompts when an exec/plugin approval is resolved from another client, such as Discord.
- Acknowledge Discord exec approval button clicks at dispatch time so slow approval handling is less likely to trip Discord's interaction timeout.
- Reuse manifest metadata during model visibility/catalog normalization so Control UI startup RPCs do not block the gateway during manual testing.
- Preserve configured model alias resolution when the selected default includes an auth profile suffix, such as `kimi@nvidia:default`.
- Treat stale resolve responses as terminal UI cleanup signals: `APPROVAL_ALREADY_RESOLVED`, `APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND`, and `unknown or expired approval id` now dismiss the prompt and refresh pending approvals.
- Add a real Dismiss/close path that hides the modal without denying or approving the backend approval; approve/deny actions target the first non-dismissed visible prompt.

Bug summary:
A web approval modal could remain open after the same approval was resolved elsewhere. Acting on that stale modal could leave the prompt showing an error instead of dismissing it. The modal also needed a local close path so the user is not trapped in the approval dialog.

During live Discord testing, the first approval click also showed Discord's "interaction failed" banner while the backend eventually resolved the approval. That was a Discord interaction acknowledgement timing problem, separate from the stale Web UI modal state.

Root cause:
On main, `exec.approval.resolved` only removed the queue item and did not clear active modal error state. The approve/deny handler also treated all resolve errors as modal errors, so backend stale-approval responses stayed visible instead of clearing the stale prompt. The modal cancel path was wired to denial, so closing was not a neutral UI action.

The first Discord approval failure happened while the gateway was under event-loop pressure. The live logs showed `exec.approval.waitDecision` taking 40794ms for the first approval and a Discord REST timeout where a 10000ms timer fired after 21886ms. The timeline for that agent turn showed 209 `plugins.metadata.scan` spans under one `reply.run_agent_turn`, totaling a large amount of synchronous work. The Discord approval button also did its acknowledgement inside `run`, so any dispatch delay left less room before Discord's interaction window expired.

Fix summary:
The approval controller now owns resolve and modal visibility cleanup. Success removes the active prompt. Stale backend errors remove it, refresh `exec.approval.list` and `plugin.approval.list`, and schedule expiry cleanup for refreshed items. A dismissed-id set lets users close the modal locally while keeping the backend approval pending; resolved/expired/refreshed state prunes that set.

The Discord approval button now opts into dispatcher-level acknowledgement and only sends initial replies when the interaction has not already been acknowledged. Stale/invalid/unauthorized messages become follow-ups after an early ACK, avoiding duplicate initial response attempts.

The model resolver now strips auth profile suffixes before checking configured default aliases and OpenRouter compat aliases. That keeps alias `kimi` pointing at its configured provider/model even when the selected default is written as `kimi@nvidia:default`.

Bug existed on main:
Yes. The worktree started from `origin/main` and the added regression failed before the fix. The rebased parent still had the same behavior: `removeResolvedApprovalRequest` only removed from `execApprovalQueue`, and `handleExecApprovalDecision` caught every resolve error as `execApprovalError`.

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed, 87 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts` passed, 14 files / 115 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts` passed, 4 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts` passed, 14 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/internal/interaction-dispatch.test.ts` passed, 3 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/internal/interactions.test.ts` passed, 10 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/interaction-dispatch.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/interactions.test.ts` passed, 18 files / 146 tests.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...changed files...` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed: no accepted/actionable findings.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` first found the model alias auth-profile regression; fixed in `4791d3eb74`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed after the fix: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ksef8gc7n5vr2w375txr8d0n`: `pnpm check:changed` passed.

Behavior addressed: stale Control UI exec/plugin approval modals after external resolution, neutral modal dismissal without resolving the backend approval, Discord exec approval button ACKs happening before approval resolution work, and configured default aliases still resolving when an auth profile suffix is present.
Real environment tested: local Vitest/tsgo/oxlint in the OpenClaw worktree, Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, and local manual Gateway with Discord + Control UI at `http://localhost:19171/`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/interaction-dispatch.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/interactions.test.ts`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed; Testbox `tbx_01ksef8gc7n5vr2w375txr8d0n` exited 0; live manual logs showed the stale modal closing on the second Discord approval flow; final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: stale resolve success/errors remove the modal, external resolved events clear active prompt errors, refreshed pending approvals expire from the UI, and Dismiss/Escape close the modal without sending a deny. Discord approval buttons now request dispatcher-level ACK before backend approval resolution. Configured default alias `kimi@nvidia:default` resolves through alias `kimi` instead of falling back to `openai/kimi@nvidia:default`.
What was not tested: a live Discord click after the dispatcher-level ACK change was not rerun; the running manual gateway still has the previous built code until restarted.
target.author: jesse-merhi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
